title: Fortinet FortiNDR Cloud
descriptionMarkdown: The Fortinet FortiNDR Cloud data connector provides the capability to ingest [Fortinet FortiNDR Cloud](https://docs.fortinet.com/product/fortindr-cloud) data into Microsoft Sentinel using the FortiNDR Cloud API
additionalRequirementBanner: These queries and workbooks are dependent on a parser based on Kusto to work as expected. Follow the steps to use this Kusto functions alias **Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud** in queries and workbooks. [Follow steps to get this Kusto functions>](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/master/Solutions/Fortinet%20FortiNDR%20Cloud/Parsers/Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud.md)

graphQueries.baseQuery: let Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud = view () { FortinetFortiNdrCloudRaw_CL }; Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsSuricata_CL'
let Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud = view () { FortinetFortiNdrCloudRaw_CL }; Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsObservation_CL'
let Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud = view () { FortinetFortiNdrCloudRaw_CL }; Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsDetections_CL'

sampleQueries.query:
Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsSuricata_CL'
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc

Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsObservation_CL'
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc

Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsDetections_CL'
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: FncEventsSuricata_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsSuricata_CL'
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: FncEventsObservation_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsObservation_CL'
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: FncEventsDetections_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsDetections_CL'
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsSuricata_CL'
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(1d)

Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsObservation_CL'
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(1d)

Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud | where Type == 'FncEventsDetections_CL'
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(1d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: MetaStream Credentials
customs.description: **AWS Access Key Id**, **AWS Secret Access Key**, **FortiNDR Cloud Account Code** are required to retrieve event data.

customs.name: API Credentials
customs.description: **FortiNDR Cloud API Token**, **FortiNDR Cloud Account UUID** are required to retrieve detection data.

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** This connector uses an enterprise application integration model via Azure Functions to securely pull logs from the FortiNDR Cloud API into Microsoft Sentinel. This deployment may incur data ingestion and compute costs. Check the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/) for details.

>**(Optional Step)** Securely store **Azure Client Secret** and **API authorization key(s) or token(s)** in Azure Key Vault. Azure Key Vault provides a secure mechanism to store and retrieve key values. [Follow these instructions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references) to use Azure Key Vault with an Azure Function App.

>**NOTE:** This connector uses a parser based on a Kusto Function to normalize fields. [Follow these steps](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/master/Solutions/Fortinet%20FortiNDR%20Cloud/Parsers/Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud.md) to create the Kusto function alias **Fortinet_FortiNDR_Cloud**.

**STEP 1 - Prerequisites & App Registration**

Ensure you have registered an identity application in your Microsoft Entra / Azure Active Directory tenant and granted it the necessary REST API execution permissions. Generate the credentials (Client Secret). You will need the **Azure Tenant ID**, **Azure Client ID**, and a valid **Azure Client Secret** to complete the custom template deployment form.

**STEP 2 - Copy Environment Configuration Parameters**

The labels below automatically expose active workspace session attributes. Copy these values now so you can easily supply them on the Custom Deployment template page:

parameters.label: Your Workspace Name (Use for workspace parameter)
Your Azure Tenant ID (Use for AzureTenantID parameter)
Your Azure Client ID and Secret from step 1

instructionSteps.description:
**STEP 3 - Deploy Ingestion Engine Components**

Click the button below to redirect to the Custom Deployment blade interface. Provide the application credentials and the target workspace parameters checked above to finish linking the pipeline securely.

parameters.title: Pipeline Parameters Verification Map
parameters.description: The deployment page will prompt you for the following customer-defined parameters. For security, the **AzureClientSecret** input will be fully masked using a secure string:

parameters.label: Azure Client ID
parameters.placeholder: Paste your Application Client ID (GUID) from Entra ID

parameters.label: Azure Client Secret
parameters.placeholder: Paste your secure Application Client Secret String

instructionSteps.description:
**Automated Deployment Controls**

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below to begin configuration assembly.

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://aka.ms/sentinel-FortinetFortiNDR-azuredeploy)
2. Assign the targeted **Subscription**, deployment **Resource Group**, and resource **Location**.
3. Input your **workspace**, **AzureTenantID**, **AzureClientID**, and **AzureClientSecret** inside the blade form inputs.
4. Validate parameters and click **Create** to execute orchestration templates.

**STEP 4 - After the connector is deployed, go to the Data Collection Rule and search for the dcr-fortinetfortindrcloud. Select Access control (IAM) and assign the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role to the integration App registration (use the display name when looking for the members).**
